[2020-11-29 00:27] silentslayers: Cool. Guess you guys don't wanna discuss more right now, or you've already said your pieces and are waiting for more dialogue
[2020-11-29 00:28] silentslayers: I personally feel the strongest about the smorcus, botch and vore nerfs, and am happy with the RR click 6 gold. Also I think I like pivot because P2 currently has a sizable winrate advantage over P1 and it appears to be pretty effective, just gotta figure out the right lifespan on it (assuming we all argree the lifespan makes sense. Only MasN seemed to think otherwise).
[2020-11-29 00:29] masn6811 (Masne98888): lifespan is okay. it seems unnecessary and inelegant, but honestly the most important thing to me is that pivot is actually in the game
[2020-11-29 00:29] silentslayers: I'm willing to be swayed on the lifespan, but I do currently feel it's cheesy getting a forcefield for example late game
[2020-11-29 00:30] silentslayers: I also feel it's likely Elyot strongly wants the lifespan, so I'm prepared to concede that even if I disagreed
[2020-11-29 00:30] masn6811 (Masne98888): it kinda is. its also kinda cute and i dont think its at all a balance issue. like the value of (G-1)*R^-17 for a turn-17 double forcefield is near 0
[2020-11-29 00:30] masn6811 (Masne98888): but yes ill concede that for that reason
[2020-11-29 00:32] silentslayers: Fair point about the value thing, but in sets where P1 *is* favored (or is equal), it's like you also get a random bonus at some point

R - red
R - rhino
R - full-turn inflation parameter
r - half-turn inflation parameter
[2020-11-29 00:34] silentslayers: We could... I don't think it's relevant to our topic right now though
[2020-11-29 00:34] masn6811 (Masne98888): honestly, im more concerned about pivot on feral/proto sets
[2020-11-29 00:34] masn6811 (Masne98888): but some sets will be p1 favored, some will be p2 favored. i think the important thing is its equal on average, and no set is too favored one way or the other
[2020-11-29 00:34] masn6811 (Masne98888): like i think minimizing squares of differences from 50% is the optimization problem here
[2020-11-29 00:35] silentslayers: just embargo feral bro 😂

[2020-11-29 00:36] silentslayers: we weren't able to find one when we voice chatted right?
[2020-11-29 00:36] silentslayers: Cause it's also a soak unit
[2020-11-29 00:36] silentslayers: i think you mentioned 4GGR but...
[2020-11-29 00:36] masn6811 (Masne98888): if its too big a problem, 4GGR is the only choice, but i dont feel its warranted yet
[2020-11-29 00:36] masn6811 (Masne98888): 4GGR is still fibroid+barrier
[2020-11-29 00:37] silentslayers: It's a crazy amount of green for constant feral absorb but maybe that's healthy
[2020-11-29 00:37] masn6811 (Masne98888): i mean, the question is if you think 5GR feral is a problem
[2020-11-29 00:37] masn6811 (Masne98888): if not, we can leave as is
[2020-11-29 00:38] silentslayers: It's probably not a problem
[2020-11-29 00:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): its certainly not as big a problem as botch/smorc/vore
[2020-11-29 00:38] silentslayers: feral sets are just lame
[2020-11-29 00:38] silentslayers: just pray you dont get feral setes
[2020-11-29 00:38] silentslayers: Sets where it's like base + 1 to be clear
[2020-11-29 00:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): if concensus is feral is worth emargoing, we do 4GGR. its still playable i bet
[2020-11-29 00:38] silentslayers: yeah it should be playable still
[2020-11-29 00:39] silentslayers: But probably the .333 hurts more than .333
[2020-11-29 00:39] silentslayers: It lines up with how proto also has GG cost
[2020-11-29 00:40] silentslayers: 2 conduits not gonna be desirable often
[2020-11-29 00:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): it will be if you're chaining
[2020-11-29 00:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): and when you're finally forced to break your chain, you get 2 forcefields
[2020-11-29 00:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): the only annoying part is getting the 2 conduits
[2020-11-29 00:41] silentslayers: yeah it'll just make it less played than if it was 5.3333GR is my arguement I guess
[2020-11-29 00:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): i honestly think 4GGR is good for play balance on second thought. since it makes natural conduit into animus more compeitive wiht natural A into C
[2020-11-29 00:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): this is true of pretty much any unit with double G in cost
[2020-11-29 00:43] silentslayers: Yeah sure, just pointing it out
[2020-11-29 00:43] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): if Feral is a big problem, then maybe test in an event? it seems like more testing is needed for most of the changes
[2020-11-29 00:44] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): also, do we have data on p1 vs p2 win rate on the 1800+ and 2000+ players? @Elyot
[2020-11-29 00:49] awaclus: my opinion on the balance changes:

I never had a problem with Dynamo or Centurion, those units were fun to play with even though they were very strong, and tentatively I feel like the new units are fine-ish, I might like them more when I've gotten used to them but so far I actually like the old units better

I like the Centrifuge, Iso Kronus and Oxide buffs a lot, especially Oxide

I agree that current Smorcus is a problem, I'm not really excited about the proposed fixes but the 6RR, RR to click Smorcus seems better than the 1R to click version and better than the current version at least

The Grimbotch nerf is FUCKING GREAT! This is what Prismata needs!

The Electrovore nerf is probably good too, it's subtle but I actually think it makes a concrete difference which makes the game more fun

I think I only got to play like one game where Arka was relevant, so I can't really speak from experience, but on paper the nerf seems good although I didn't think the current one was a problem either

no opinion on Pivot so far

I really think if there's a balance update, it should include a Cryo nerf, not because it's too strong (which it might also be but I'm not very confident about that) but because it's too annoying

my opinion on the balance changes:

I never had a problem with Dynamo or Centurion, those units were fun to play with even though they were very strong, and tentatively I feel like the new units are fine-ish, I might like them more when I've gotten used to them but so far I actually like the old units better

I agree that current Smorcus is a problem, I'm not really excited about the proposed fixes but the 6RR, RR to click Smorcus seems better than the 1R to click version and better than the current version at least

The Grimbotch nerf is FUCKING GREAT! This is what Prismata needs!

The Electrovore nerf is probably good too, it's subtle but I actually think it makes a concrete difference which makes the game more fun

I think I only got to play like one game where Arka was relevant, so I can't really speak from experience, but on paper the nerf seems good although I didn't think the current one was a problem either

no opinion on Pivot so far

[2020-11-29 02:11] apooche: just to break out the ones I think are interesting since there is no previewer for the long message:
Electrovore: Not a huge fan of it. I get what scenarios the change helps in, but I see vore as a unit that lets you sacrifice its engineer fuel because the 2 soak is decent. I think this version is less interesting because protecting the engineers is too much more important when vore is only 1 soak. I feel like the "ideal" change balance wise would be to make it fragile, but it doesn't make sense because it isn't green.

Arka: Is this a buff? It got quite a bit cheaper as an attacker. It has to absorb twice before it even starts to fall behind the current version in profit. I like it at a 4/6, but I would give more thought to the cost. At high inflation or when there is another absorber you're just buffing the best unit in the game.

Gaussite pivot: I prefer ideas like Aetherpulse and Vendium Scrap where you get a pure gold opening helper. I get that the problem p1 faces a lot of time is in green, but there are also plenty of green sets where p1 is actually advantaged (Synthesizer, Dynamo, some variants of feral, Zemora), and it feels kind of unfair that this streches their advantage there. Have we looked at P1 openings with triple blue units and synth? Can we make pivot require that you own a conduit? I think it is a bit more fair that way. It probably helps more often than hurts as it is, just feels dirty sometimes.
[2020-11-29 02:15] silentslayers: Vore can be pretty oppressive though, something needs to be done. The 1 HP is definitely better balance, just less interesting.
[2020-11-29 02:16] silentslayers: The fragile honestly wouldn't fix it's brokenness much at all, though it is a cute idea (if it didn't also have the lacking green problem)
[2020-11-29 02:16] silentslayers: If anyone has other vore ideas I'd like to hear them

[2020-11-29 10:25] silentslayers: __**More Unit Change Considerations (Discussed with MasN):**__

**Fission Turret:** Reduce 1 lifespan, reduce 1 energy cost on click.

Notes: Fission turret shouldn't be such an efficient attacker when it's a small green unit. Especially considering it's efficient even when it's not clicked. The reduction of energy needed should help make this nerf feel a bit less dramatic.

**Lancetooth:** Cost is now 8BX instead of 6BXX.

Notes: Lancetooth is too strong of a blue attacker when denying 2 absorb. Having efficient attack ramping and being in the color of defense simultaneously felt too easy of a tech choice. Lancetooth is still a good purchase when denying 1 absorb, but should be less desirable to go for and spam than currently, and should punish high economy lines less often.

**Scorchilla:** Scorchilla's HP is now 2.

Notes: Scorch is a pretty set dominating unit, because of its efficiency as an initial attacker, and its ability to deny absorb. The fact that it can also absorb 2 felt like icing on the cake it didn't need, so this should make it a bit less powerful. No strong opinion on whether fragile or not.

**The Wincer:** Costs 1 extra red.

Notes: Chrono filter wincer is a 2 unit combo which leads to many autowins and brainless openers. This will immediately remedy that. It also reduces the strength of mobile animus + wincer, which is also very strong. Specific combos aside, it also slightly nerfs Wincer, although not really by that much since the red is usually not spent on great things. You now wouldn't be able to fit in a rhino alongside your Wincer purchase, which might give some rushes a better chance. Not fitting in the tarsier doesn't feel too disastrous since Wincer players really like drones to be able to continue to spend their large amount of tech pre and post firing Wincer.

messages.author: silentslayers
messages.content:
__**More Unit Change Considerations (Discussed with MasN):**__

**Fission Turret:** Reduce 1 lifespan, reduce 1 energy cost on click.

Notes: Fission turret shouldn't be such an efficient attacker when it's a small green unit. Especially considering it's efficient even when it's not clicked. The reduction of energy needed should help make this nerf feel a bit less dramatic.

**Lancetooth:** Cost is now 8BX instead of 6BXX.

Notes: Lancetooth is too strong of a blue attacker when denying 2 absorb. Having efficient attack ramping and being in the color of defense simultaneously felt too easy of a tech choice. Lancetooth is still a good purchase when denying 1 absorb, but should be less desirable to go for and spam than currently, and should punish high economy lines less often.

**Scorchilla:** Scorchilla's HP is now 2.

Notes: Scorch is a pretty set dominating unit, because of its efficiency as an initial attacker, and its ability to deny absorb. The fact that it can also absorb 2 felt like icing on the cake it didn't need, so this should make it a bit less powerful. No strong opinion on whether fragile or not.

**The Wincer:** Costs 1 extra red.

Notes: Chrono filter wincer is a 2 unit combo which leads to many autowins and brainless openers. This will immediately remedy that. It also reduces the strength of mobile animus + wincer, which is also very strong. Specific combos aside, it also slightly nerfs Wincer, although not really by that much since the red is usually not spent on great things. You now wouldn't be able to fit in a rhino alongside your Wincer purchase, which might give some rushes a better chance. Not fitting in the tarsier doesn't feel too disastrous since Wincer players really like drones to be able to continue to spend their large amount of tech pre and post firing Wincer.
